bullets.text: Combined ratio of 85.4 for Q2; year-to-date combined ratio of 84.3, substantially better than the industry's reported 94.2 for Q1 2004.
Frequency rates down across all coverages versus 2003; bodily injury severity trending at 2–5% annualized and physical damage severity near flat, supporting rate stability.
Net premiums written grew 11% for the quarter; management expects new business growth to slow as low frequency reduces consumer shopping motivation.
Concierge-level physical damage claims service met key operating specifications and is ready for expansion; four new Florida service centers under evaluation plus scouting in four other states.
Progressive became the number-one auto insurer in Rhode Island and Vermont; discussions with New Jersey regulators ongoing regarding potential market entry.
Customer service restructured into three aligned groups — agency customers, direct customers, and independent agents — to better serve distinct needs and improve targeted efficiencies.
Market described as transitioning from hard to soft; management emphasized that best service at lowest price is a winning formula through all phases of the insurance cycle.
